commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=1e874523c689027edfcfe1b72e46d07873de8ff8)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Windows-only CLI respawn plan that prepends `--stack-size=8192` and updates respawn planner tests.

PR surface: Source +18, Tests +29. Total +47 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: the linked issue provides Windows crash steps and current main still has the Windows respawn bypass. The PR-introduced argv-ordering problem is also source-reproducible because `entry.ts` normalizes Windows argv only after `buildCliRespawnPlan()` runs.
commands.review_followup: Human handling is needed because the PR has a protected label, missing contributor real-behavior proof, and a Windows argv-ordering finding that should be fixed before merge.

target.title: fix(cli): add Windows stack-size respawn
target.body:
## Summary
- add a Windows-only respawn path that launches the CLI with `--stack-size=8192`
- preserve existing Windows behavior for CA cert and warning-suppression respawns
- skip the respawn when the user already provided a `--stack-size` exec arg
- pin non-Windows respawn baseline tests to Linux so Windows test runs exercise the new path deterministically

Fixes #62055.

## Validation
- WSL/Linux: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/entry.respawn.test.ts --reporter=dot` -> 15 tests passed
- WSL/Linux: `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/entry.respawn.ts src/entry.respawn.test.ts`
- WSL/Linux: `git diff --check`
- WSL/Linux: `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/entry.respawn.ts src/entry.respawn.test.ts`
- Windows 11 / Node v22.22.3: `node node_modules\vitest\vitest.mjs src/entry.respawn.test.ts --reporter=dot --run` -> 15 tests passed

Note: commit is SSH-signed locally and verified with `git log --show-signature -1` after rebasing onto current `main`.
